**Serotonin 5HT1 Receptor**: Acts mainly through the PLC pathway which generates diacylglycerol (DAG), activating PKC; this mechanism does not involve any direct action upon potassium channels as its primary effector process.

**Angiotensin II Type I (AT1) Receptors**: Engage mostly in intracellular cascades involving DAG and IP3 production after binding, ultimately elevating cytosolic Ca²+ concentrations rather than influencing K⁺ channels.

Both Serotonin 5HT1 and Angiotensin II Type I show minimal if no direct impact on potassium ion regulation compared to other listed options.
